notes: Server-independent storage architecture; Aberdeen says 1998 is not year of SANs; mainstream expected 1999+.
File-serving server-independent storage; simpler than SAN; gaining adoption.
100MB/sec storage networking; hubs and switches not mature enough in 1998; pilots recommended.
Internal storage connectivity in legacy disk arrays and tape libraries; ESCON/SCSI can integrate with SAN.
Now commonplace on LAN servers due to lower disk prices; complements but does not replace tape backup.
Essential for archiving and disaster recovery; rapidly changing technologies (Super DLTtape, LTO).
Next-generation tape format; goal terabyte/hour backup at reasonable cost.
Quantum next-generation DLT tape technology announced; competing with LTO.
Automated tiering of data from online to near-online to offline storage.
Key differentiator over raw hardware; backup/restore, capacity planning, data sharing, supervision.
Aberdeen's term for any storage a business user accesses over a network; advocates treating storage as system.
IBM proprietary alternative storage channel; listed as connectivity option alongside Fibre Channel and SCSI.
IBM mainframe channel connectivity; can be integrated into SAN alongside newer Fibre Channel.
